what_it_does: A React (browser-Babel, no imports) Gallery screen for the platform kit: a title, an ActionToolbar, an Images section with a grid/list view toggle and 15 selectable media tiles, and a Videos section with 5 video tiles. Tile selection and the grid/list view are interactive via React state.
observations: Aliases React's useState as useState_g (likely to avoid collisions in a shared browser bundle)
Defines MediaTile (selectable, optional video play overlay) and the main Gallery component
Uses real interactivity: a Set-based selection state (sel) initialised to {0,6,8}, a toggle handler, and a grid/list view toggle (view state)
References an external ActionToolbar component (not defined here) — a cross-file dependency within the kit
Renders 15 image tiles and 5 video tiles via Array.from(...).map
Video play glyph is an inline SVG with a hardcoded rgba(31,26,18,.08) fill on its circle
Uses cv- classes from platform.css (cv-tile, cv-tile-grid, cv-section-divider, cv-view-toggle, cv-screen-title)
Self-registers via window.Gallery at the end; no module export
A couple of inline style objects (marginLeft auto, marginTop 36)
standouts: Unlike the static Calendar screen, this one carries genuine interaction (tile selection + view toggle)
The useState_g alias signals awareness of a shared single-bundle global scope
conventions: Follows the kit pattern of composing cv- classes over restating styles
BREAKS the token convention with one hardcoded rgba(31,26,18,.08) literal in the SVG overlay
concerns: Depends on ActionToolbar which is defined elsewhere — load order/availability matters in the shared bundle
The grid/list view toggle sets state but the tile rendering does not change between grid and list (toggle is visual-state only, no list layout)
notes: An interactive Gallery mockup screen for the platform kit — selectable image tiles and a grid/list toggle — composing kit classes and an external ActionToolbar, registered on window.
questions: Where is ActionToolbar defined and how is its load order guaranteed before Gallery mounts?
Is the grid/list toggle meant to actually re-lay-out the tiles, given it currently only changes the active button?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly matches — it is a Gallery screen and renders one, with working selection; the 'list' view is declared but not yet rendered differently.
intention_signals: Aims to show a working media gallery screen — selectable images and videos with a view toggle — recognisable and lightly interactive for review.
novelty: The deliberate useState_g aliasing to coexist in a shared global React bundle.
contribution: Contributes the Gallery screen's structure and interaction model for the platform kit: a selectable media-tile grid, a grid/list view toggle, and separate Images/Videos sections composed from kit classes.
summary_for_embedding: Interactive React Gallery screen for the platform UI kit — selectable image/video media tiles and a grid/list view toggle composed from platform.css classes and an external ActionToolbar, registered on window.
intention_for_embedding: To present a lightly interactive media-gallery screen for the platform kit for recognition and review.
